microsoft_ft: Served as the **technical bridge** between Azure engineering and enterprise partners (Databricks, Walmart, SAP, NetApp), owning end-to-end integration of resilience validation workflows into partner environments and driving **$14M+ in business impact** across deployments.

Led 0→1 development of an internal **Resilience Automation Platform**, defining product requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that reduced operational toil by 30% and transitioned execution to a scalable, repeatable deployment model.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability, delivering a rack-level drill program in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate**, surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a new standard for continuous production validation.

Directed **14 cross-org recovery executions** under executive visibility, including serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract, coordinating across engineering, ops, and partner stakeholders.

Built an internal **AI agent** to automate drill planning workflows, cutting planning cycle time by 39% and increasing drill capacity by 21% — directly translating field observations into product improvements that scaled team throughput.

Standardized integration workflows across 45+ annual resilience drills by authoring runbooks, ETL data-validation procedures, and cross-team escalation protocols, enabling self-service execution and reducing dependency on senior engineers.
microsoft_2023: Drove **product adoption** for AI-powered code generation workflows across 14 Azure service teams, running hands-on demos and training sessions that embedded the tooling into daily engineering practice and saved **37 engineering hours monthly**.

Conducted 11+ discovery interviews with Azure service teams to surface critical integration gaps, directly influencing the product roadmap to prioritize **intent-based YAML generation** — translating field context into a shipped feature.

Improved **data integrity and retrieval** by implementing rigorous metadata standards and migrating documentation to an AI-powered semantic search tool, cutting lookup time by 83% and enabling faster onboarding for new engineering partners.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched regions 28% faster by aligning 140+ teams on a unified automation prioritization framework, resolving competing roadmaps and unblocking cross-functional deployment execution.

Led customer-environment discovery with 20+ service teams, identifying **81 hours of manual toil** per region launch and building a phased automation roadmap that targeted the highest-impact integration bottlenecks first.

Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** to track operational toil across 140+ teams, giving leadership real-time visibility into automation gaps and enabling data-driven prioritization of deployment support resources.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by owning the legacy OS migration strategy, mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations, and coordinating cross-functional rollout with IT, ops, and field engineering teams.

Facilitated Agile sprint ceremonies and managed the deployment backlog, resolving **40+ high-priority tickets** to stabilize the developer intake process and keep the migration on schedule across distributed customer environments.

Drove alignment across IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and reducing manual intervention during production rollouts.

Documented integration dependencies, rollback procedures, and environment-specific configurations into a reusable runbook, enabling field teams to execute future deployments **self-sufficiently** without escalating to senior engineers.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by redesigning the end-to-end proposal and invoicing lifecycle for 200+ monthly engagements, deploying a new CRM workflow that gave the team real-time visibility into customer pipeline and reduced manual follow-up.

Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and boosted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing the website and Google Business profile to improve discoverability among underserved local customers.

Conducted profitability analysis across **1,000+ project records** to identify and seal margin leaks, delivering pricing model recommendations to leadership that directly improved gross margin on future engagements.

tailoring_notes: 'Technical bridge' (JD: 'be the bridge') woven into microsoft_ft bullet#1 — directly mirrors Promise's framing of the FDE role as the connector between engineering and agency/utility partners.
'Self-service deployment model' and 'operational toil' (JD: 'problem-solve in the wild', 'level up the product') used in microsoft_ft bullets#2 and #6 to frame platform work in FDE vocabulary.
'Field observations into product improvements' (JD: 'you will have first-hand context... build it') used in microsoft_ft bullet#5 to show the FDE feedback loop Cyrus already lives.
'Customer-environment discovery' (JD: 'dive into customer environments') used in microsoft_2022 bullet#2 to reframe intern discovery work as forward-deployed customer instinct.

'Self-sufficiently without escalating' (JD: 'curiosity & grit — you don't wait for someone to tell you the answer') used in amazon_robotics bullet#4 to signal the autonomous, documentation-first mindset Promise values.
'Underserved local customers' (JD: mission — 'low-income families', 'dignity') subtly woven into pro_painters bullet#2 to signal mission alignment with Promise's civic-impact orientation.
